claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to understand a bit about your situation. 

**First, let me ask about your child:**

1. **How old is your child?** (or if you have multiple children, let's start with the first one and their age)

2. **What is your child's name?** (This helps me keep track if you have multiple children)

Child Benefit eligibility depends significantly on the child's age:
- Children **under 16** generally qualify automatically (subject to other conditions)
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** typically don't qualify

Please share your child's age and name, and we'll work through the eligibility requirements together.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hi! Thanks for helping me with this.

I have one child - Alex, who's 6 years old. He lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me work through the eligibility assessment for Alex.

Since Alex is 6 years old (under 16), let's continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Since Alex is 6 years old and lives at home with you, let me continue with the next question:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

For a 6-year-old child, this would typically be "yes" - they wouldn't be in paid employment. Can you confirm this is the case for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex doesn't work at all. He's only 6, so he's just at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, that makes sense! Now the next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

This means - is Alex receiving Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit in his own name (not as part of your household claim)?

For a 6-year-old child, this would typically be "yes" (meaning he's NOT receiving these benefits in his own right). Can you confirm Alex doesn't receive ESA or Universal Credit in his own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's just a regular 6-year-old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Next question:

**Is Alex in any type of apprenticeship?**

Since Alex is 6 years old and just at school, I'm assuming he's not in an apprenticeship program. Can you confirm that's correct?

(For context: certain types of apprenticeships can affect Child Benefit eligibility for older teenagers, but this wouldn't apply to a 6-year-old.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship - he's far too young for that! He's just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Now we need to check your residency status. This is an important requirement for Child Benefit.

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You would qualify if ANY of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (this is the most common route)
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and meet certain conditions (working, studying, have sufficient resources, etc.)
4. You're a Crown servant living abroad or in certain qualifying countries

**Do you normally live in the UK?** This would be the typical situation for most applicants.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now let's check responsibility for Alex.

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

You mentioned earlier that Alex lives at home with you, so this should be straightforward. You would meet the responsibility requirement if ANY of these apply:

1. **You live with Alex** (which you've already mentioned is the case)
2. You contribute to Alex's upkeep (paying at least the amount of Child Benefit towards their care)
3. You pay maintenance under a court order or agreement

Since you've already told me that Alex lives at home with you in Manchester, that means you meet the responsibility requirement. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to ask:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

For example:
- Alex's other parent
- Another family member
- Anyone else

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at a time. If someone else is currently claiming, you would need to agree with them to transfer the claim to you.

Is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now I need to check Alex's living situation in a bit more detail.

**Which of the following best describes Alex's current situation?**

- Is Alex currently **in hospital**?
- Is Alex currently **in care** (looked after by the local authority)?
- Is Alex **fostered**?
- Or is Alex living **normally at home** with you?

You mentioned Alex lives at home with you, so I'm assuming it's the last option - living normally at home. Is that correct, or does any of the other situations apply?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - Alex is living normally at home with me. He's not in hospital, care, or fostered.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Just one more question:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, is the council paying for Alex's living costs or upkeep? 

Since Alex lives at home with you and attends regular primary school, I'm assuming the council is not paying for his accommodation or maintenance. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's correct - the council isn't paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News! You Are ELIGIBLE for Child Benefit!

**Result:** You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.

### Why You're Eligible:

✅ **Alex qualifies as a child** - He's 6 years old (under 16), not in paid work, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship

✅ **You meet residency requirements** - You normally live in the UK (Manchester)

✅ **You're responsible for Alex** - Alex lives at home with you

✅ **No competing claims** - Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

✅ **No local authority support** - The council isn't paying for Alex's accommodation or maintenance

---

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate

### Important Information:

⚠️ **High Income Child Benefit Charge:** If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay back some or all of the Child Benefit through the tax system. However, you can still claim it to protect your National Insurance credits.

💷 **Payment:** Child Benefit is normally paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits).

### How to Apply:

Visit: **https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim**

If you have any questions about the application process, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) Alex is 6 years old, which is under 16 - automatic qualification age; (2) Alex is not in paid work; (3) Alex does not receive benefits in his own right (ESA or Universal Credit); (4) Alex is not in an apprenticeship; (5) The claimant normally lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements; (6) The claimant lives with Alex, meeting the responsibility requirement; (7) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex lives normally at home (not in hospital, care, or fostered); (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 6-year-old son Alex. Alex meets all child qualification criteria as a child under 16 living at home with no disqualifying circumstances. The claimant meets all claimant requirements including UK residency, responsibility for the child (living together), and no competing claims or local authority support.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

All eligibility criteria are met.
